commit.message:
Reorder CLAUDE.md sections by fundamentality

Move architecture constraints before coding/docstring guidelines
and development commands after them, so sections flow from most
fundamental (structure, architecture) to most specific (commands,
process).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement all 7 consolidation opportunities

Extract PersistentConfig base class for config persistence, add
self-description methods to ToolCall subtypes, standardize widget
factory signatures, freeze event dataclasses, make PermissionManager
sync (removing aiofiles dependency), convert relative imports to
absolute, and remove module docstrings from __init__.py files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add architecture constraints and consolidation docs

Document recurring patterns, invariants, and symmetries across the
codebase as topic-scoped constraint files so coding agents can load
only what is relevant. Add consolidation opportunities as a separate
backlog. Update AGENTS.md with absolute import convention and pointer
to architecture constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix inaccurate module descriptions in project structure docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Link to section headers in SDK docs

Refactor permissions to use Pydantic model with default rules

Replace raw list storage in PermissionManager with a PermissionsConfig
Pydantic model that provides validated defaults (pytools listing and
.freeact/** reads). Add init() method following the Config/TerminalConfig
lifecycle pattern: load from file if present, otherwise save defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add keyboard shortcut hints below input field

Show "ctrl+q: quit" below the input field, switching to
"ctrl+q: quit  esc: interrupt" during agent activity.
Also adds the ctrl+q binding to quit the app.

Update banner with blue-purple color gradient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add granular permission system with typed tool calls and pattern matching (#78)

* Add granular permission system with glob patterns, shell command approval, and editable approval bar

Rewrite PermissionManager to support fnmatch glob patterns (*, ?) organized
into ask/allow tiers with session/always persistence. Add shell command
extraction from IPython cells using TransformerManager + AST parsing, with
composite command splitting. Extend ApprovalRequest with a shell flag and
add pre-execution shell command approval in the agent layer. Update the
approval bar to display editable patterns and route allow_always/allow_session
through domain-aware APIs. Fix PermissionManager eagerly creating .freeact/
directory in constructor (defer to save()) to avoid interfering with
Config.init() bootstrapping. Migrate old permissions.json format automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refine permission system: move shell logic to shell.py, add ApprovalBar properties, simplify approval flow

Move suggest_shell_pattern and _KNOWN_SUBCOMMAND_TOOLS from permissions.py
to shell.py to colocate shell logic. Add early-exit optimization in
extract_shell_commands. Change a/s keys to save_rule flow that enters edit
mode before saving. Expose editing and pattern as public properties on
ApprovalBar. Use Literal types for domain parameters. Simplify pre-approval
check in _handle_approval. Hoist shell imports to module level in core.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Require Python >= 3.13 for full_match support

- Update docs: configuration.md (config-only), cli.md (approval prompt UI), api/agent.md (ToolCall hierarchy)
- Update internal docs: architecture and testing references

commit.message:
Fix test_main_init mock to match updated load_dotenv signature

The .env discovery fix (37eb55e) changed `load_dotenv()` to
`load_dotenv(find_dotenv(usecwd=True))`, but the test mock was a
no-arg lambda. Patch both `find_dotenv` and `load_dotenv` to
accept the new call signature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix .env file discovery when running via uvx

find_dotenv() defaults to frame-walking from the caller's file location,
which resolves to the uvx cache directory instead of the user's working
directory. Use find_dotenv(usecwd=True) to search from cwd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add agent turn cancellation via Escape key (#76)

Implement cooperative cancellation using an asyncio.Event checked at
phase boundaries in Agent.stream(). Escape in the terminal calls
Agent.cancel(), which sets the event and interrupts the ipybox kernel.
The stream stops at the next boundary and yields a Cancelled event.
Subagent kernels are interrupted via a monitor task. Orphaned tool calls
receive synthetic ToolReturnPart entries to preserve conversation
coherence.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add prefix matching for skill and file pickers (#75)

* Add prefix matching for skill picker and file picker

Typing characters in the skill picker (/) or file picker (@) now jumps
to the first matching item instead of requiring arrow key navigation.
The search is case-insensitive with fallback to shorter prefixes so a
non-matching keystroke doesn't lose the current position. In the file
picker, the search is scoped to children of the current expanded
directory or siblings of the current node.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tests for file picker sibling-scoped prefix matching

Verify that prefix search on an expanded directory only matches its
children (not ancestor nodes like /tmp) and that prefix search on a
file node only matches siblings in the same directory.

- Tests: remove unnecessary filesystem I/O from skill helper, extract

as prompt preprocessor. Add skill tag expansion (<skill> tags with

Split core.py into focused modules:
- events.py: all event dataclasses (AgentEvent through ApprovalRequest)
- _supervisor.py: _ResourceSupervisor async lifecycle utility
- _subagent.py: _SubagentRunner event bridging via queue

Restructure tool execution in core.py:
- Add _ToolExecResult for uniform handler results
- Extract _dispatch_tool() as pure match/case dispatcher
- Flatten _execute_tool() with early returns for unknown/rejected

Add session persistence with SessionStore (#61)

* Add session trace persistence with SessionStore

Persist pydantic-ai message history incrementally as JSONL envelopes
under .freeact/sessions/<uuid>/, one file per agent id. Support session
resume via --session-id CLI argument. Subagent traces are written for
audit but not rehydrated on resume.

Also clean up test setup by removing manual .freeact/ directory creation
(Config now handles it), use _ConfigPaths for path consistency in tests,
and standardize on @pytest.mark.asyncio.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move agent_id from Config to Agent constructor

agent_id is per-instance state, not shared configuration. Removing it
from Config and adding it as an Agent constructor param (defaulting to
"main") better matches how persistence always uses "main" for the main
agent and how subagents already received unique IDs at creation time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update CLAUDE.md with header, key deps, and session persistence docs

Rename freeact/agent/session.py to freeact/agent/store.py to clarify
that the module handles storage, not session lifecycle. Add persistence
documentation under the Agent API section in sdk.md, add --session-id
to cli.md, update configuration.md directory structure, and create
examples/persistent_agent.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename subagent_task.json to subagent.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move init_config() into Config.init() classmethod

Consolidates config scaffolding into Config itself, backed by a shared
_ConfigPaths dataclass for path definitions. The init method is now async
to run blocking I/O in a separate thread via ipybox.utils.arun.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor Agent constructor to accept Config instead of 13 separate parameters

Consolidate all agent parameters (agent_id, images_dir, execution_timeout,
approval_timeout, enable_subagents, max_subagents, kernel_env) into Config,
loaded from config.json. Move ${VAR} resolution for mcp-servers and kernel-env
into Config at load time. Remove --execution-timeout and --approval-timeout
CLI flags. Simplify subagent creation via Config.for_subagent().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add {generated_rel_dir} and {plans_rel_dir} placeholders to prompts and skills

Replace hardcoded .freeact/generated and .freeact/plans paths with template
placeholders in system prompts and skill templates. System prompts render
the placeholder at load time via Config. Skill templates render placeholders
in init_config() before copying to .freeact/skills/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Consolidate .freeact/ path definitions into Config

Config now owns all .freeact/ paths as properties (plans_dir,
generated_dir, search_db_path), eliminating the scattered GENERATED_DIR
constant. Both basic and hybrid MCP servers receive paths via PYTOOLS_DIR
env var. Removes ipybox_register_mcp_server from Agent since MCP server
registration is an administrative/setup concern, not a runtime agent tool.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move generated tool sources into .freeact/generated/

Consolidate mcptools/, gentools/, and mcpparse/ directories under
.freeact/generated/ to keep generated artifacts within the config
directory. Adds PYTHONPATH to kernel env so imports resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Disable pytools sync/watch for subagent hybrid search servers (#59)

Subagents were spawning their own hybrid search MCP server processes
that redundantly synced the index (wasting embedding API calls) and
started duplicate file watchers against the shared SQLite database.
Override PYTOOLS_SYNC and PYTOOLS_WATCH to "false" in the subagent's
copy of the pytools config.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor Agent config and MCP server management (#58)

* Rename _include_subagent_task_tool to with_subagents

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move MCP server creation from Config into Agent

Config now purely loads raw server configs (dict[str, dict[str, Any]])
while Agent owns the creation of pydantic-ai MCPServer instances via
private _create_mcp_servers(). The hardcoded _EXCLUDED_FILESYSTEM_TOOLS
is replaced by a generic excluded_tools key in servers.json config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename Agent MCP-related attributes for clarity

- mcp_server_factory param -> mcp_servers (raw config dicts)
- self._mcp_servers (instances) -> self._mcp_server_instances
- self._tool_servers -> self._tool_mapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add parallel test execution via pytest-xdist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename with_subagents to enable_subagents

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename Agent id parameter to agent_id with default "main"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Defer MCP env var replacement to Agent, unify server loading in Config

Config._load_mcp_servers and _load_ptc_servers merged into a single
_load_servers(key) that validates env vars but keeps placeholders.
Actual replacement moved to Agent._create_mcp_servers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Separate internal configs from user configs in .freeact/

Move system prompts to package resources and define internal MCP servers
(pytools, filesystem) as constants in config.py. Replace servers.json with
a unified config.json that holds tool-search setting alongside user server
definitions. Internal server configs merge with user overrides, where user
entries take precedence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Run integration tests in parallel in CI workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Default HOME env var in kernel environment

Pass the host HOME environment variable to the ipybox kernel
when not explicitly provided in kernel_env, so tools and code
running in the sandbox can resolve home directory paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump ipybox dependency to >= 0.7.4

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add execution_timeout and approval_timeout params to Agent (#55)

* Add execution_timeout and approval_timeout params to Agent

- execution_timeout: Maximum time for code execution (default 300s).
  Approval wait time is excluded from this budget.
- approval_timeout: Timeout for PTC approval requests (default None).

Requires ipybox>=0.7.3 for timeout excluding approval wait.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add --execution-timeout and --approval-timeout CLI options

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix flaky timeout test for slow CI environments

Increase execution_timeout from 3s to 10s and PTC approval delay from
5s to 15s to ensure the test passes on slower CI environments while
still validating that approval wait time is excluded from the timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix BM25 search failing on queries with special characters (#54)

FTS5 interprets characters like commas, parentheses, colons, and quotes
as query syntax, causing parse errors. Fix by quoting each token to
treat them as literals.

commit.message:
Prefer shell commands over filesystem tools for directory operations (#53)

Filter out directory-related tools from the filesystem MCP server and update
system prompts to encourage using shell commands via `!` prefix in ipybox
instead. This simplifies the toolset by leveraging shell commands for listing,
finding, and system tasks while keeping filesystem tools focused on file I/O.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add hybrid BM25/vector tool search (#52)

## Summary

- Add hybrid search as an alternative to basic category browsing for tool discovery
- Combine BM25 (keyword) and vector (semantic) search using Reciprocal Rank Fusion
- Support real-time index updates via file watching
- Configure via `--tool-search hybrid` CLI option or `init_config(tool_search="hybrid")`

## Implementation

**Core modules** (`freeact/agent/tools/pytools/search/hybrid/`):
- `database.py`: SQLite with FTS5 and sqlite-vec for hybrid storage
- `embed.py`: pydantic-ai embedder wrapper for query/document embeddings
- `extract.py`: AST-based docstring extraction from tool files
- `search.py`: BM25, vector, and hybrid search with RRF fusion
- `index.py`: Orchestrates sync between tool directories and search index
- `watch.py`: File watcher for real-time index updates
- `server.py`: FastMCP server exposing `search_tools` tool

readme_html:
Freeact is a lightweight agent harness and CLI tool that acts by executing code and shell commands in a stateful execution environment. It uses code actions to improve itself and its tool library.

It generates Python APIs for MCP servers and calls their tools programmatically ("code mode") instead of JSON. This enables tool composition in code actions in a single inference pass.

Freeact has a tiny core and uses sandboxed IPython kernels to execute both Python code and shell commands in a uniform way. Execution runs locally with fine-grained approval of actions.

[!NOTE] Supported models: Freeact supports any model compatible with Pydantic AI, with gemini-3-flash-preview as the default. See Models for provider configuration and examples.

Documentation

  • 📚 Documentation
  • 🚀 Quickstart
  • 🤖 llms.txt
  • 🤖 llms-full.txt

Capabilities

Capability Description
Code actions Freeact agents act via Python code and shell commands. This enables tool composition and intermediate result processing in a single LLM inference pass.
Local execution Freeact executes code and shell commands locally in an IPython kernel provided by ipybox. Data, configuration and generated tools live in local workspaces.
Sandbox mode IPython kernels optionally run in a sandbox environment based on Anthropic's sandbox-runtime. It enforces filesystem and network restrictions on OS-level.
MCP code mode Freeact calls MCP server tools programmatically1) via generated Python APIs. This enables composition of tool calls in code actions with much lower latency.
Tool discovery Tools are discovered via category browsing or hybrid BM25/vector search. On-demand loading frees the context window and scales to larger tool libraries.
Tool authoring Agents can create new tools, enhance existing tools, or save code actions as reusable tools. This captures successful experience as executable knowledge.
Agent skills Skills give agents new capabilities and expertise based on agentskills.io. They compose naturally with code actions and agent-authored tools.
Subagent delegation Tasks can be delegated to subagents, each using their own sandbox. It enables specialization and parallelization without cluttering the main agent's context.
Action approval Fine-grained approval of code actions and (programmatic) tool calls from both main agents and subagents. Enables human control over potentially risky actions.
Session persistence Freeact persists agent state incrementally. Persisted sessions can be resumed and serve as a record for debugging, evaluation, and improvement.

Usage

Component Description
Agent SDK Agent harness and Python API for building freeact applications.
CLI tool Terminal interface for interactive conversations with a freeact agent.

1) Freeact also supports MCP server integration via JSON tool calling, but the recommended approach is programmatic tool calling.
